assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md defines two epics: 'Epic 1: TC-9006: trustify-backend' (Tasks 1, 2, 3) and 'Epic 2: TC-9006: trustify-ui' (Tasks 4, 5, 6, 7). Grouping strategy is explicitly stated as 'by-repository (from CLAUDE.md Hierarchy Configuration)'.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic 1 summary: 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', Epic 2 summary: 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Both follow the exact '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' format.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Task Summary table shows: Tasks 1-3 (trustify-backend) have Parent Epic 'TC-9006: trustify-backend'; Tasks 4-7 (trustify-ui) have Parent Epic 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Each task's Repository section in its individual file confirms the repository assignment matches.

assertion_results.text: Incorporates links are created from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks)
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Incorporates Links section (lines 29-34): 'TC-9006 incorporates Epic "TC-9006: trustify-backend"' and 'TC-9006 incorporates Epic "TC-9006: trustify-ui"', followed by explicit note '(Links go from Feature to Epics, not from Feature to individual Tasks.)'

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1-3, frontend tasks 4-7. Task 4 (frontend) depends on Tasks 1 and 2 (backend). Task 5 depends on Task 4. Task 6 depends on Task 5. Task 7 depends on Tasks 5 and 6. All cross-epic dependencies flow from lower-numbered backend tasks to higher-numbered frontend tasks.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files verified: Task 1 has Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (main), Description, Files to Create (7 files), Files to Modify (2 files), Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria (5 items), Test Requirements (3 items). Tasks 2-7 similarly contain all required sections. Every task has at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create.

assertion_results.text: Epics are created with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic 1: 'Issue type: Epic (level 1)', 'Parent: TC-9006 (Feature)'. Epic 2: 'Issue type: Epic (level 1)', 'Parent: TC-9006 (Feature)'. Both use the exact issue type name 'Epic' and reference TC-9006 as parent.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files have a single-value Repository section: Tasks 1-3 have 'trustify-backend', Tasks 4-7 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files contain: '## Target Branch' (all set to 'main'), '## Description' (substantive multi-sentence descriptions), '## Acceptance Criteria' (checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (checkbox items). Verified against task-description-template.md which requires these sections.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files end with properly formatted digest lines. Verified each has exactly 64 lowercase hex characters: Task 1: sha256-md:d5f2eab17ec9d85cec2871ec8aae1b609d4734a2b70b2c0a68145fbc0e3e7643, Task 2: sha256-md:cd2451c7df92cbfcf9fc84692520db4e88970461377bd824bc8b453f9ca8f6c4, Task 3: sha256-md:2631d7593ac0f73cdded8720dd808cc34d91f5b52608707a29b58c098f703520, Task 4: sha256-md:0f8bcc37b349d18ed57bbd7a545be96faeddd4005b02506d8a499651d45f7298, Task 5: sha256-md:637253659a669220021fbc84268d08e77716615bee7a72a666895a3ea86afcf1, Task 6: sha256-md:cc663710b19f3f1ad98defffef806ec6cd42241d3bcaddfc5570eb147177262a, Task 7: sha256-md:0f5ff06cd35eb7d8da596596336f8f9c828f9d31d0ae2699601b46088c8ecebe. All use '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' prefix.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention rationales follow the prescribed format. Examples: Task 1: 'Applies: task creates `modules/fundamental/src/remediation/endpoints/summary.rs` matching the convention's Rust module file scope.' Task 4: 'Applies: task modifies `src/api/rest.ts` matching the convention's .ts API file scope.' Task 5: 'Applies: task creates `src/pages/RemediationDashboardPage/RemediationDashboardPage.tsx` matching the convention's .tsx page file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature priority is Major (not Undefined). impact-map.md 'Inherited Fields' section states 'Priority: Major (propagated to all tasks and Epics)'. The 'additional_fields (per task and Epic)' section includes '"priority": {"name": "Major"}' in the JSON block that applies to all tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: Feature has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0. CLAUDE.md has no explicit fixVersion scope config, so it defaults to 'both'. impact-map.md states 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated to all tasks and Epics; fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. The additional_fields JSON includes '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]' for all tasks.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md contains an 'Inherited Fields' section (lines 69-72) explicitly documenting: 'Priority: Major (propagated to all tasks and Epics)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated to all tasks and Epics; fixVersion scope defaults to "both")'. The additional_fields JSON block (lines 76-82) also shows both values.
